Title: Diverse MarR bacterial regulators of auxin catabolism in the plant microbiome

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Complementation with iadCDE versus iadDE; gene deletion of iadCDE; IAA degradation assay; Arabidopsis root growth assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Absence slows IAA degradation and reduces ability to fully rescue Arthrobacter CL28-induced RGI, although iadDE alone can still degrade IAA.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. iadC is not sufficient alone but enhances the rate of IAA degradation when co-expressed with iadDE. 2. Inclusion of iadC with iadDE improves complementation of CL14 ΔHS33 and is required for wild-type-level rescue of Arthrobacter CL28-dependent RGI. 3. The gene likely provides electron transfer support to the IadDE dioxygenase complex.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: IMG gene ID suffix 70 within locus 26436136##
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as a ferredoxin subunit; other ferredoxins in the genome may partly compensate when iadC is absent.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis root environment/rhizosphere; bacterial culture supernatant
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA supplementation; Arabidopsis duo-association with Arthrobacter CL28
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Including iadC with iadDE may be necessary for maximal auxin-degradation performance in engineered strains used in complex microbiome contexts.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances IAA degradation rate and is needed for robust metabolic signal interference in bacterium-induced RGI context.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is an auxin signal controlling plant root development; faster degradation improves suppression of root growth inhibition.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: IAA inducible within the iad operon

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. iadD together with iadE is necessary and sufficient for IAA degradation in Variovorax paradoxus CL14. 2. Deletion of iadDE abolishes IAA degradation and abolishes reversion of root growth inhibition (RGI) caused by IAA or Arthrobacter CL28 on Arabidopsis. 3. Heterologous expression of iadDE complements IAA degradation in both V. paradoxus CL14 ΔHS33 and Escherichia coli.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is an auxin signal controlling plant root development; bacterial degradation interferes with auxin signaling and prevents root growth inhibition in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly catalyzes the initial committed oxidative degradation of target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated from the iad locus in the presence of IAA; derepressed in ΔMarR_73 background.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis root environment/rhizosphere; bacterial culture supernatant
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: IMG gene ID suffix 69 within locus 26436136##
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: iadDE alone confers slower IAA degradation than iadCDE; slower degradation is insufficient to fully revert Arthrobacter CL28-dependent RGI to wild-type CL14 levels.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rieske/non-heme iron aromatic-ring hydroxylating dioxygenase alpha subunit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA supplementation; Arabidopsis mono-association or duo-association with Arthrobacter CL28; M9 succinate medium
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with IadE as an aromatic-ring hydroxylating dioxygenase required for oxidative conversion of IAA in the iad-like degradation pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by IAA in V. paradoxus CL14 cultures; higher expression in ΔMarR_73.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA degradation and enables metabolic signal interference that prevents auxin-dependent root growth inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step for IAA degradation in the iad-like pathway; minimal necessary catalytic module established.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; complementation; heterologous expression in E. coli; IAA quantification; Arabidopsis root growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: iad-like aerobic IAA degradation branch to anthranilic acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: iadD is a weak homologue of iacC and acts in a pathway distinct from iac-like loci, which instead proceed toward catechol.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. iadE together with iadD is necessary and sufficient for IAA degradation in Variovorax paradoxus CL14. 2. Deletion of iadDE abolishes IAA degradation and loss of metabolic signal interference on Arabidopsis. 3. Expression of iadDE restores IAA degradation in mutant backgrounds and in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is an auxin signal controlling plant root development; degradation mitigates root growth inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly catalyzes the initial required degradation step of target metabolite with IadD
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: IAA inducible within the iad operon; derepressed in ΔMarR_73.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis root environment/rhizosphere; bacterial culture supernatant
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: IMG gene ID suffix 68 within locus 26436136##
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: iadDE-only complementation gives slower degradation than iadCDE and weaker rescue of bacterium-induced RGI.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA supplementation; Arabidopsis mono-association or duo-association with Arthrobacter CL28; M9 succinate medium
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts with IadD in an aromatic-ring hydroxylating dioxygenase complex required for IAA oxidation in the iad-like pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by IAA in V. paradoxus CL14 cultures; higher expression in ΔMarR_73.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA degradation and auxin metabolic signal interference.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step for IAA degradation in the iad-like pathway; minimal necessary catalytic module established.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; complementation; heterologous expression in E. coli; IAA quantification; Arabidopsis root growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: iad-like aerobic IAA degradation branch to anthranilic acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: iadE is the partner subunit of IadD in the catalytic dioxygenase complex.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein-ligand ITC; crystal structures; DNA-binding ITC with operator oligo; knockout; complementation; RNA-seq; IAA degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is a plant auxin regulating root development; its bacterial degradation suppresses auxin-driven root growth inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-responsive transcriptional repression; IAA binding derepresses the iad operon
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses downstream iad locus genes including iadD/iadE in the absence of IAA; IAA binding releases repression and activates locus expression indirectly by derepression.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as a homodimeric MarR-family regulator
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: IMG gene ID suffix 73 within locus 26436136##
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA supplementation; Arabidopsis root growth assay context; M9 succinate medium
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion increases IAA degradation rate, indicating regulatory braking of pathway expression; no direct plant tradeoff reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MarR_73 binds IAA with high affinity (~0.41 μM). Crystal structures defined key IAA-contacting residues Ser28, His32, Arg46 and hydrophobic indole-coordinating residues. DNA binding was demonstrated using MarR_73 S28A because native protein likely retained ligand from expression host.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MarR_73 is the primary IAA-responsive repressor of the Variovorax iad locus. 2. It binds IAA with high affinity and binds an operator upstream of iadA. 3. Deletion causes constitutive upregulation of downstream iad genes and faster IAA degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: iad operon genes downstream of iadR, including iadA and iadDE module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory module for engineering bacteria with inducible auxin-degradation capacity and enhanced microbiome-mediated root development protection.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE210968; metabolomics, JGI portal ID 1340427
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis root environment/rhizosphere; bacterial culture supernatant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in the auxin degradation locus; locus expression is IAA inducible and derepressed in ΔMarR_73.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Metabolite-dependent derepression by the substrate IAA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary IAA-sensing transcriptional gatekeeper controlling expression of the iad catabolic locus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses IAA degradation in the absence of auxin; loss or ligand binding increases IAA catabolism and supports auxin signal interference.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter/operator between iadR and iadA; inducible by IAA via derepression

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein-ligand ITC; knockout; RNA-seq; IAA degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is a plant auxin whose degradation modulates root growth inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Putative transcriptional repression/amplification of iad locus response; exact mechanism unclear
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Deletion mainly upregulates gene 51 independent of IAA and appears to amplify iad locus upregulation in response to IAA.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MarR-family dimer; direct complex evidence not beyond family inference
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: IMG gene ID suffix 50 within locus 26436136##
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No IAA binding was detected and no DNA target was identified. Nevertheless, deletion increases IAA degradation rate, indicating a regulatory role in the locus.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MarR_50 is a secondary MarR-family regulator in the iad locus. 2. It does not detectably bind IAA, and its direct DNA target remains unresolved. 3. Deletion increases IAA degradation and alters transcript patterns, suggesting modulatory repression of the pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Possibly gene 51 and/or auxiliary iad locus genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to relieve regulatory constraints and increase bacterial auxin-degradation performance, but mechanism requires clarification.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis root environment/rhizosphere; bacterial culture supernatant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Located in auxin degradation locus; transcriptomic effects evident upon deletion.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Secondary modulatory regulator affecting IAA degradation rate
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Unknown; not IAA by direct binding under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion increases IAA degradation rate, implying negative regulation of auxin catabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus promoters; specific operator not identified
